question.content: For the cantilever-beam and rigid-cylinder configuration in the accompanying image, beam AB has length l and bending stiffness EI, is fixed at A and free at B, and carries a downward concentrated force F at B. After loading, the segment near A stays in smooth contact with a rigid cylindrical surface of radius r, so the contact segment has constant curvature 1/r; the remaining segment is detached from the cylinder and deforms elastically. In this loading case, the tangent at A remains horizontal, and F > EI/(rl), so there is a boundary point C: segment AC is in contact with the cylinder while segment CB is free from contact. Use C as the connection between the contact segment and the free-bending segment, and compute the deflection at free end B. Answer format: a single algebraic expression. Use the variable names specified in the problem and ASCII characters only; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Method: contact-geometry plus short-cantilever kinematics. Use the cylinder to locate point C and its tangent, then treat CB as a shorter cantilever mounted on that moving and rotating base.
step_1.key_point: First determine the base state at C, then add the local bending of BC.

step_3.content: Curvature matching at the release point gives x=BC because the moment at C is F*x and the contact curvature is fixed by the cylinder. So the separation point is determined before any end-deflection calculation.
step_3.key_point: Point C comes from curvature equality, not from end-deflection partitioning.

step_5.content: The contact arc AC sets two kinematic data at C: its vertical drop y_C and its tangent rotation θ. Then B inherits a rigid-body shift y_C+θ*x before the free segment BC adds its own local bending.
step_5.key_point: Use C as a moving base carrying translation and rotation.

step_7.content: Add the short-cantilever tip deflection F*x^3/(3EI) to the geometric base motion at C, then substitute x=EI/(Fr). This produces exactly the same closed-form result as solution1.
step_7.key_point: Total end deflection equals base kinematics plus local BC bending.
